title: Trusting the System
fullRewardHint: Answer all {{total}} choices correctly to earn the full reward.
reflectionPrompts: How do regulations protect your money in banks?
What should you look for when choosing a financial institution?

stages.prompt: You fear banks will 'take your money.' What's the truth?

options.label: Banks freely misuse deposits
options.reflection: Banks are heavily regulated and misusing deposits would violate numerous laws and regulations.

options.label: Banks hide how they use your money
options.reflection: Banks provide statements and are required to be transparent about how they handle deposits.

options.label: Banking is inherently unsafe
options.reflection: Banks are among the most regulated industries with multiple safeguards to protect deposits.

options.label: Banks are regulated and monitored
options.reflection: Exactly! Banks operate under strict regulations and oversight to protect customer deposits.

stages.prompt: What protects your money in banks?

options.label: The bank's physical size
options.reflection: Physical size doesn't protect your money; regulatory frameworks and deposit insurance do.

options.label: Deposit insurance and regulatory oversight
options.reflection: Exactly! Deposit insurance and regulatory oversight provide key protections for your money.

options.label: The bank manager's verbal promise
options.reflection: Verbal promises aren't sufficient protection; regulatory frameworks and insurance provide real security.

options.label: The bank's location in a safe area
options.reflection: Location doesn't protect your money; regulatory protections and insurance do that.

stages.prompt: How often are banks audited and monitored?

options.label: Never, banks self-regulate
options.reflection: Banks undergo regular audits by both internal and external agencies, as well as regulators.

options.label: Only once per year
options.reflection: Banks are monitored much more frequently than annually through various oversight mechanisms.

options.label: Regularly by regulatory authorities
options.reflection: Exactly! Banks are subject to regular audits and supervision by regulatory authorities.

options.label: Only by customer complaints
options.reflection: Banks have formal regulatory oversight that goes beyond customer complaints.

stages.prompt: What happens if a regulated bank faces financial trouble?

options.label: Customer deposits are protected up to certain limits
options.reflection: Exactly! Deposit insurance schemes protect customer deposits up to specified limits during bank failures.

options.label: Customers immediately lose all their money
options.reflection: Regulatory frameworks exist to protect customers, often including deposit insurance and bank resolution processes.

options.label: Nothing happens to customer deposits
options.reflection: While deposit insurance provides protection, there are formal processes that occur during bank troubles.

options.label: Customers always lose some money
options.reflection: Deposit insurance typically covers customer deposits up to certain limits during bank failures.

stages.prompt: Why are regulated institutions safer than informal storage?

options.label: They cost less to use
options.reflection: The safety of regulated institutions comes from oversight and insurance, not lower costs.

options.label: They have regulatory oversight and customer protections
options.reflection: Exactly! Regulatory oversight, licensing requirements, and customer protections make regulated institutions safer.

options.label: They're more convenient to access
options.reflection: While regulated institutions may be convenient, their safety comes from regulatory protections.

options.label: They always offer higher returns
options.reflection: Safety of regulated institutions comes from oversight and protections, not necessarily higher returns.
